STR: +10 DEX: +10 HP: +30
Attack: +10 HP Regen +2
WT: 1.0 Size: SMALL
Class: WAR PAL RNG SHD BRD ROG BST BER
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Belt of the Great Turtle

AC: 6
STR: +10 DEX: +10 HP: +30

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Iceclad Ocean
NPC Name

Zone(s) Found In:

Iceclad Ocean


Uploaded December 26th, 2024 by iventheassassin
Send a correction
« Previous 1 2 Next »
Post Comment
spawn + kill
QuoteReply
# Jan 30 2007 at 5:07 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591925919179
33 posts
This guy spawns in the ocean next to the Gnoll camps by ToFS.
His rare drop is the + 2 regen no lvl recomm or req. belt.
He is about lvl 58-60.
He summons not very often like once every 30 seconds he summons.. but he does it twice in a few seconds then waits again enough time to get back.. heal and dot.
Im a lvl 70 druid Vt/potime geared because I just got back after a long break.
He was cake.. and i finished him with 100% health and 60% mana after redotting twice 5 nukes and a few heals.
Dropped the belt (YAY) both map parts.. sections of shield.. earring no boots.
All i wanted whats section and belt so im happy.
I have no idea how often he spawns but i usually go check every 3 days or so and hes up.

RE: Why is the level to attain different from level of mob??
QuoteReply
# Mar 09 2004 at 1:59 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593361703291
15 posts
You've never killed a yellow or red con mob before?

It's not necessary to match the level of a mob in order to kill it. If a group of 58's can take Lodizal down then the level to attain is, justifiably, 58.

BST Usable...
QuoteReply
# Dec 15 2003 at 7:23 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591788552764
7 posts
This belt is BST usable, although I could have sworn it wasn't during the summer. I remember duo'ing Lodi with a chanter, and the belt dropped, finally, and the chanter had a WAR toon and I a BST toon. Well I could have sworn it wasn't BST usable then, so I passed on it (this was like July or August maybe?). Well I just solo'd that big turtle today and the belt dropped again and it is now BST usable, so I'm confused. Oh well, better late than never.

Oh these are going for about 4-5k max on Test. Maybe that's why it wasn't marked BST usable then, something goofy with Test Server /shrug

My Belt

# Nov 23 2003 at 3:19 AM Rating: Default

Got my belt on 11/13/03!
Really happy Sk. What happened was I woke up in the morning, friend was camping Lodi, then he spawned! Got a Shammy Bst, whipped him up in under a minute between the 3 of us.

Then, he spawned later, about 10 hours I would say, so we thjis got 5 people, I ended up dieing to save the group, which didn't matter cause I got my belt. =D
Well, just telling what happened to me.

# Jun 07 2003 at 3:40 AM Rating: Default

Scholar
34 posts

Ac low .... ah well if im getting hit im not doing my job right ( and if soloing the ac isnt tooo low)
10 str - increase atk and damage .. cool
10 dex - increase riposte, crits, procs, bow damage , bow doubles , bow crits ... coool
aura of battle increase atk by 10 and perm stackable 2 point regen .. nice

For a Ranger or Rogue this is excellent - its preety damn fine for any melee - so if you are lucky enough to get to roll on it .... congratulate whoever gets it - :) one day it may be you ...

QuoteReply
# Apr 22 2003 at 3:28 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591768191932
View User Forum
Sage
118 posts
6AC, ok that works. 10 STR, oh yeah my swift blade of Zek like. 10 DEX, Oh my Light Velium bow relly like! 30HP my sore body can use! Aura of Battle, make those health breaks shorter! And 10 ATK hum...... whats that! :) I think this is the only item I have with added ATK so this is not something I ever looked at. But hey extra anything, ok give it to me!!! Being that I only solo for now this is great all around for my little 32 Ranger! I wouldn't complain about added stats, unless they don't proc until 49 or something. Man I hate that!

14 attack for 61 Pal
QuoteReply
# Mar 03 2003 at 11:46 AM Rating: Default

View User Forum
4 posts
Just a confirmation that this item yielded 14 attack to a level 61 Paladin. As far as stacking goes, it of course stacks with our yaulp, while the "grim aura" effect of the goblin skull earring is overwritten by yaulp.

I advise this increased attack for us do-gooders because a little bit of attack when magnified by slay undead 3 means a greatly increased damage output against those of undead origin.

__DEL__1592758246482
69 posts
You're all wrong. It adds 10 raw attk, but the number you see is your modified attk, based on class, str, etc.

QuoteReply
# Feb 10 2003 at 6:32 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592250918896
8 posts
imo (and i really mean imo) 10 atk is absolutly overrated. players are a bit crazy when it comes to ac, atk or haste. i mean if you have 1000 atk... do you really think 10 points will change much? i dont. a 39 shadowknight can buff his atk by at least 30 points with his own spells - if 10 atk really mean this much that would make him kind of uber. so i think 10 atk is overrated. i have to admit that this belt is not bad at all though - nice stats, 2 hp regen and 10 atk are nice but dont think just the 10 atk will win a fight for you.

Edited, Mon Feb 10 18:02:41 2003
▲ Page top
RE: this 10 atk is overrated
QuoteReply
# Mar 04 2004 at 3:35 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591761666008
34 posts
ok mr Thornkiss you are a bit silly imo, sure 10atk is not great, but worn atk stacks up to 250 as has been mentioned in this post and worn atk stacks with your self buffed atk as well so you get 15 items that all added 10 atk and your getting 150 worn attack and that is base so you won't see 150 atk, you'll see closer to 200 off that. and no this belt is not uber but when you start stacking effects like this then eventually you are going to see nice bonuses to your melee ability. by your logic why bother lvling to lvl 2 cuz it's not all that much better then lvl 1. and y would anyone waste their time leveling to 3 that isn't much better then 2... see where this is going?

# Nov 21 2003 at 3:08 AM Rating: Decent

▲ Page top
RE: this 10 atk is overrated
QuoteReply
# Mar 15 2003 at 7:05 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591786829646
31 posts
It depends what class you are. Rogues for example collect ATK increases the way casters collect mana items. A plus mana item typically only gives a small amount extra but if you try to have as many slots have plus mana items as possible then it adds up. Similarly with attack bonuses like this one. For a warrior it might not be much, but for a rogue they will try to have as many ATK bonuses as they can on as many pieces as possible and it adds up.
▲ Page top
RE: this 10 atk is overrated
QuoteReply
# Feb 18 2003 at 6:51 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592253812287
74 posts
Well, 10 atk is a very nice thing to have, but yea youre right - its not ALOT. Yes, your attack can be buffed up through str buffs and some self-cast spells if you can (im not sure if there are any atk spells that like a rogue could get), but the numbers can be a big difference in the "end game". The 10 atk difference between 1490 and 1500 atk is very big apparentely and numbers around there, i dont have that much yet so i dont know exactly. I love this belt, the stats, and the effect - and seeing the 10 more atk is nice to look at :) Im a rogue so the more atk i can get the better! Well, the belt is nice - no more should be said about the quality.

is atk bonus wrong
QuoteReply
# Feb 01 2003 at 8:55 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1595780163160
1 post
just got this today woot. on a 54 bst my attack went from 1014 to 1040.
▲ Page top
question
QuoteReply
# Jan 12 2003 at 11:06 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592758270846
26 posts
Is 10 attack really that much? Obv better than none, but what sort of boost would a person get from this? I dont find the 2 regen to be a big deal at all, but am curious about the ATK.
▲ Page top
RE: question
QuoteReply
# Apr 02 2003 at 7:10 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591767968112
Scholar
27 posts
The effect of ATK is relatively small compared to the noisiness of the random number generator, which means that there isn't any conclusive parse data (at least, not that I've been able to find) that establishes the value of ATK and the general shape of the curve (for example, does 10 marginal points of ATK have a bigger impact at ATK 1000 or ATK 1400?). The best read I've found on the topic is http://forums.interealms.com/ranger/showthread.php?threadid=9616

With all those caveats aside... the most common rule of thumb is that 10ATK = 1% damage increase. And while there is a point of diminishing returns on ATK that is dependent on the mob, any mob where your ATK is "soft-capped" is a mob that you wouldn't be wasting your time killing in the first place. And this is why ATK rules: as you get to be around level 60, it is relatively easy to hit the (soft) AC and (hard) stat caps - but more ATK keeps letting you do more damage.
▲ Page top
AoB as permanent effect stacking.
QuoteReply
# Nov 21 2002 at 12:18 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591770052860
Scholar
93 posts
If Aura of Battle is a permanent effect, it stacks with everything. I mean EVERYTHING.

There's only one exception alleged that I am aware of: some say there is a hard cap on how much regen and atk you can get from items. Something along the lines of +300 atk, +30 regen. Most players will never get near it.
▲ Page top
RE: AoB as permanent effect stacking.
QuoteReply
# Jan 03 2003 at 2:11 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591771471616
14 posts
There is a hard cap of 30hp/tick on worn regen.

There is a "soft" cap of 250 on +atk, but no hard cap known. The 250 number doesn't actually affect worn +atk, but instead the atk bonus of the Avatar buff procced from Sleeper's Tomb weapons. Gonna use a little addition to illustrate.

1-150 +atk = everything is normal
151+ +atk = you get less than 100+atk from eff:Avatar on your weapons. You only get enough to bring you up to +250 total.

Avatar (and all variations thereof) cast on you by a shaman is unaffected by this cap, and will always add the full +atk ammount.

Hope everyone could follow. Anyhow, all this is extremely unlikely to impact more than 1% of any server.

▲ Page top
RE: AoB as permanent effect stacking.
QuoteReply
# Jan 08 2004 at 4:25 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591771471616
14 posts
edit wouldn't work on such an old post :P

Worn regen is capped at 35/tick, now

Worn atk is capped at +250
▲ Page top
RE: AoB as permanent effect stacking.
QuoteReply
# Dec 01 2002 at 5:51 AM Rating: Good
KalRox
View User Forum
282 posts
there are 2 kinds of aura of battle in game. the effect on this belt is worn, so it gives no buff icon, so it is stackable with everything.there are other items with aura of battle click,so it will give a buff icon,(inspect, it will show up as "triggered",i think there is a pally/sk shield somewhere with it),in this case, aura of battle might give some stackability problems ( but i think only with other aura of battle click items).

Roxas Nitehowler
60warder of illumanati
MM
yup wearer of a turtle belt.
and yes, this is an old picture of the belt.

hunter Roxas Nitehowler
Plainswalker of illumanati
MM, EQ

hunter Roxas
WoW retired

for any melee, haste is essential. all other things come after that. if you already have a haste item in some other slot, then this belt rocks for sure. but again, haste first )
